title: Cumulative displaced plastic waste as a result of Chinese import ban
subtitle: Projected cumulative displaced plastic globally as a result of the Chinese ban on recycled plastic waste imports. This is based on projected scenarios of a 100%, 75% or 50% ban on global imports of recycled plastic waste to China.
caption:
Cumulative displaced plastic waste as a result of Chinese import ban

Projected cumulative displaced plastic globally as a result of the Chinese ban on recycled plastic waste imports. This is based on projected scenarios of a 100%, 75% or 50% ban on global imports of recycled plastic waste to China.

This line chart displays three projected scenarios for cumulative displaced plastic waste globally from 2018 to 2030, measured in million tonnes. The vertical axis ranges from 0 to 120 million tonnes, while the horizontal axis marks years in two-year increments. Three dotted lines represent different ban scenarios: a 100% import ban (blue), a 75% import ban (brown), and a 50% import ban (orange). In 2018, all three lines begin near the bottom of the chart, between 0 and 20 million tonnes. By 2030, the 100% ban line reaches approximately 110 million tonnes, the 75% ban line reaches about 80 million tonnes, and the 50% ban line reaches roughly 55 million tonnes. The lines diverge steadily over time, with the 100% ban scenario consistently positioned highest on the chart, followed by the 75% ban, then the 50% ban. Each line maintains a roughly linear upward trajectory across the period shown. The spacing between the lines widens as the years progress, indicating increasing differences in cumulative displaced waste under each scenario. No data points are marked for individual years beyond the start and end of the projection period. The chart does not include any additional annotations, gridlines beyond the major axis ticks, or country-specific breakdowns. All values are presented as global aggregates under each hypothetical ban level.
